tool_calls.name: retrieve_information
tool_calls.args: {"prompt":"What does the SpaceX S-1 filing disclose about the voting rights of Class A and Class B common stock? Specifically, how are directors elected - does Class B vote as a separate class to elect a majority of directors? How are the remaining directors elected? What is the voting power of each class on general shareholder matters? Please extract the exact language from {{spacex_s1}} about the dual-class voting structure and director elections."}

tool_call.name: retrieve_information

Specifically, how are directors elected - does Class B vote as a separate class to elect a majority of directors? How are the remaining directors elected? parsed_args.prompt: What does the SpaceX S-1 filing disclose about the voting rights of Class A and Class B common stock? Specifically, how are directors elected - does Class B vote as a separate class to elect a majority of directors? How are the remaining directors elected? What is the voting power of each class on general shareholder matters? Please extract the exact language from {{spacex_s1}} about the dual-class voting structure and director elections.

tool_output.output:
# Voting Rights of Class A and Class B Common Stock in the SpaceX S-1

## 1. Voting Power Per Share

The filing establishes a dual-class structure with asymmetric voting power:

> "Subject to the terms of our charter, each holder of our Class A common stock is entitled to **one vote per share**; each holder of our Class B common stock is entitled to **ten votes per share**; and the holders of our Class C common stock will have **no voting rights**."

---

## 2. Election of Directors — Class B Elects a Majority (51%)

Yes, Class B votes **as a separate class** to elect a majority of the board. The exact language states:

> "With respect to the election of directors, our charter will provide that (i) holders of our Class B common stock, **voting separately as a class**, are entitled to elect **51% of the total number of authorized directors** (rounded up to the nearest whole number) **for so long as any shares of Class B common stock remain outstanding**; and that (ii) holders of all classes of our voting common stock, voting together as a single class, are entitled to elect the remaining directors (the '**Common Stock Directors**')."

This is reiterated in the controlled-company disclosure:

> "Under the terms of our charter, the holders of our outstanding Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, will have the right to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors, rounded up to the nearest whole number (the '**Class B Directors**')."

---

## 3. Election of Remaining Directors

The remaining directors (the "Common Stock Directors") are elected by **all classes of voting common stock voting together as a single class**:

> "Holders of Class A and Class B common stock, **voting together as a single class**, will elect the remaining members of our board (the 'Common Stock Directors')."

---

## 4. Removal and Vacancy Filling

### Class B Directors

> "Class B Directors may be removed **with or without cause** by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of Class B common stock, **voting separately as a class**. Vacancies occurring with respect to the Class B Directors, including as a result of newly created directorships on the board, may be filled at any time by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, **or by the remaining Class B Directors, and not any other persons**, subject to the terms of our charter."

### Common Stock Directors

> "Common Stock Directors may be removed with or without cause by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of voting common stock, **voting together as a single class**. Vacancies occurring with respect to the Common Stock Directors, including as a result of newly created directorships on the board, may be filled at any time by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of voting common stock, voting together as a single class, **or by the remaining directors**."

---

## 5. Voting on General Shareholder Matters

On matters other than director elections and the protective provisions described below, **both classes vote together as a single class**:

> "Generally speaking, with respect to matters to be voted on by shareholders of the Company, the holders of all classes of our voting common stock will **vote together as a single class**."

The cover page footnote confirms:

> "Class A shareholders and Class B shareholders will vote together as a single class on all matters to be voted on by shareholders under our charter, **except** the holders of our Class B common stock will have the right to elect a majority of our board and have certain other voting rights as a class."

---

## 6. Class B Protective Provisions (Separate-Class Votes)

Beyond director elections, Class B also has separate-class voting rights on certain fundamental corporate actions:

> "In addition to any other required vote, under our charter, the approval of the Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, is required to approve (1) any amendment to our charter that would make any change in the rights, powers, preferences and privileges of the Class B common stock (including with respect to Class B Directors); and (2) certain combinations, mergers or sales, as described in our charter."

The filing further notes an **opt-out** from statutory class votes:

> "Otherwise, classes of common stock will not be entitled to any separate class votes, as our charter will provide for an **opt-out from class votes** that would otherwise be required under the [TBOC]."

---

## 7. Elon Musk's Protected Leadership Roles

The filing grants Musk special removal protection tied to Class B approval:

> "Mr. Musk will only be subject to removal from the board and from his Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the board leadership positions with the approval of the holders of at least a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock, **voting separately as a class**."

---

## 8. Preferred Stock Precedence

All voting rights are subject to future preferred stock grants:

> "Notwithstanding the above, each of the voting rights described above will be subject to the rights that may be granted in the future to the holders of any one or more series of preferred stock, as applicable."

---

## Summary Table

| Feature | Class A | Class B | Class C |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Votes per share** | 1 | 10 | 0 |
| **General matters** | Vote together with Class B as single class | Vote together with Class A as single class | No voting rights |
| **Director elections** | Vote with all classes to elect remaining ("Common Stock Directors") | **Vote separately as a class to elect 51% of directors ("Class B Directors")** | No voting rights |
| **Removal of Class B Directors** | No separate right | Majority of Class B, voting separately as a class | N/A |
| **Removal of Common Stock Directors** | Majority of voting common stock, voting together as single class | Participates in single-class vote | N/A |
| **Charter amendments affecting Class B rights** | No separate class vote | **Separate class approval required** | N/A |
| **Certain mergers/combinations** | No separate class vote | **Separate class approval required** | N/A |
